mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 6.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 102.5 signals a strengthening supply chain that reduces production costs and increases corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 102.5 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 15900 signals a strengthening economy that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 15900 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 15900 and a 3-month increase in housing starts above 900 signals a strengthening economy and housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 15900 and HOUST > 900 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5 signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in initial unemployment claims below 250000 signals a strengthening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 250000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 103 signals a strengthening supply chain that reduces production costs and increases corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 103 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
